prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
# A Chronicle of the Quarterly Reckoning

Verily, I have witnessed the great Lord Quarterly present his accounting to the assembled masses, wherein he proclaimed that the ethereal subscriptions—those invisible tributes paid monthly by distant merchants—did increase by the sum of twenty-three percent, though whether this be measured in crowns or in the abstract mathematics of your modern age, I cannot discern. The keepers of the ledger did speak much of something called "churn rate," which I confess I took to be literal butter-making gone awry until they displayed strange glowing rectangles showing lines both ascending and descending like the very towers of Babel. In mine own day, we measured prosperity by the weight of grain in the storehouse, yet these SaaS-merchants seemed most troubled by a phantom figure called "customer acquisition cost," as though customers were wild beasts to be hunted rather than subjects who owed their lord fealty. Most perplexing was the celebration at document's end, when the assembly did rejoice despite learning they had spent more gold than they had earned—a paradox that surely defies both scripture and reason.
